rationale: Add a lightweight procedural guard config to cap CLI arg length and prefer file-based payloads, reducing Windows argument-length failures without touching code.

edits.content:
{
  "schema": "brainflow.procedural.cli_arglen_guard.v1",
  "max_arg_chars": 7000,
  "prefer_file_payload": true,
  "file_payload_fields": ["prompt", "context", "messages", "input"],
  "notes": "On Windows, avoid long command lines by writing payload to temp files and passing file paths instead."
}

risks: If downstream code ignores this config, behavior remains unchanged.
expected_benefit: Lower rate of Windows CLI argument-length errors and more reliable LLM/tool invocation.
